message.content: Olá! Por favor, verifique o hasness descrito no CLAUDE.md e continue com a próxima tarefa, ao final, emita um alerta e aguarde - ignore o contexto na pasta ./packages

content.thinking:
O usuário está pedindo para verificar o harness descrito no CLAUDE.md e continuar com a próxima tarefa. Vou seguir o protocolo:

1. Ler claude-progress.txt para entender o estado atual
2. Ler features.json para encontrar a próxima feature (passes: false)
3. Implementar uma feature
4. Testar visualmente com Playwright MCP
5. Commit após passar
6. Atualizar claude-progress.txt

Vou começar lendo o claude-progress.txt primeiro.

content.text:

{
  "project": "Metro Integrated Planning System (MMS)",
  "version": "0.3.0",
  "lastUpdated": "2025-12-16",
  "product": {
    "entry": "apps/canvas",
    "description": "Production React app with semantic zoom (Vite + React 18 + R3F + BlueprintJS 6)"
  },
  "reference": {
    "entry": "design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
    "description": "Standalone POC for visual reference only"
  },
  "priorityQueue": {
    "description": "Features ordered by execution priority. Execute in order, respecting blockedBy dependencies.",
    "note": "Priority 0 = immediate, Priority 1 = high, Priority 2 = after dependencies"
  },
  "features": [
    {
      "id": "HARNESS-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Configure Playwright MCP for headless without focus stealing",
      "steps": [
        "Research Playwright headless configuration options",
        "Update MCP settings for headless mode",
        "Test that browser windows don't steal focus during visual tests",
        "Document configuration in CLAUDE.md"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "EXECUTE FIRST - Harness improvement to prevent browser windows from interrupting user's parallel work during visual tests."
    },
    {
      "id": "QA-PLAN-001",
      "category": "harness",
      "priority": 0,
      "description": "Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking (planning mode)",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking enabled",
        "Research QA best practices for canvas/WebGL applications",
        "Design protocol to enforce QA tests AFTER visual tests",
        "Define QA checklist based on UX-AUDIT-001 discoveries",
        "Update harness hooks to enforce QA step",
        "Document in docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "PLANNING TASK - Use planning mode with deep thinking to design mandatory QA testing protocol. QA tests should run after visual tests pass. Informed by UX research."
    },
    {
      "id": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "category": "research",
      "priority": 1,
      "description": "UX Best Practices Deep Research & Documentation",
      "steps": [
        "Enter planning mode with deep thinking",
        "Study UI patterns: Canva, Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Android",
        "Document all mouse events (click, dblclick, drag, hover, wheel, context)",
        "Document all touch events (tap, pinch, swipe, long-press)",
        "Research cross-platform differences (macOS/Windows/Linux scroll)",
        "Research hardware differences (trackpad vs mouse)",
        "Create docs/UX_RESEARCH.md with all findings",
        "Update harness to preserve context across sessions"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "HARNESS-001",
      "notes": "CRITICAL RESEARCH - Deep dive into UX patterns from industry leaders. Informs F118-F121 implementation."
    },
    {
      "id": "F118",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Momentum/inertia panning (velocity-based drag)",
      "steps": [
        "Track drag velocity during mouse movement",
        "On mouse release, continue panning with velocity",
        "Apply friction to gradually stop",
        "Test on macOS, Windows (if available)",
        "Match Google Maps/iOS feel"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "UX enhancement - drag release continues with velocity and friction, like Google Maps/iOS."
    },
    {
      "id": "F119",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Smooth zoom UX (iOS/Google Maps feel)",
      "steps": [
        "Add easing to zoom transitions",
        "Implement zoom momentum",
        "Add pinch-to-zoom support for touch devices",
        "Smooth out zoom steps",
        "Test across different input devices"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "UX enhancement - improve zoom feeling with easing, momentum, pinch-to-zoom support."
    },
    {
      "id": "F120",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Double-click to zoom in (BUG FIX)",
      "steps": [
        "Add dblclick event handler to canvas",
        "Calculate click position in world coordinates",
        "Zoom 2x centered on click position",
        "Animate zoom transition smoothly",
        "Respect MAX_ZOOM limit"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "BUG: Double-click zoom not implemented in production app (was working in POC F004)."
    },
    {
      "id": "F121",
      "category": "ux",
      "priority": 2,
      "description": "Cross-platform scroll/input sensitivity normalization",
      "steps": [
        "Research deltaY values across browsers/OS",
        "Normalize scroll sensitivity for macOS/Windows/Linux",
        "Handle trackpad vs mouse differences",
        "Implement adaptive sensitivity based on input type",
        "Test on multiple platforms"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "UX-AUDIT-001",
      "notes": "REQUIRED: Normalize scroll sensitivity across all OS and hardware types."
    },
    {
      "id": "F116",
      "category": "performance",
      "priority": 3,
      "description": "Performance optimization (60fps)",
      "steps": [
        "Profile with Chrome DevTools",
        "Maintain 60fps during pan/zoom",
        "No memory leaks on extended use",
        "InstancedMesh optimizations",
        "Frustum culling for off-screen tasks"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "F110",
      "notes": "Critical for production quality. Can be worked on in parallel with UX features."
    },
    {
      "id": "F117",
      "category": "canvas",
      "priority": 4,
      "description": "Full visual parity with POC reference",
      "steps": [
        "Compare screenshots with design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html",
        "All 3 LOD layers match visually",
        "Pan/zoom behavior identical",
        "Sidebar and timeline sync correctly",
        "Conflict indicators match"
      ],
      "passes": false,
      "blockedBy": "F116",
      "notes": "Final validation - POC as visual reference"
    },
    {
      "id": "F001",
      "category": "core",
      "description": "Canvas renders and initializes without errors",
      "steps": [
        "Open design/reference/semantic-zoom-poc.html in browser",
        "Canvas displays with header, sidebar, and main area",
        "No JavaScript errors in console",
        "Mock data generates 50 trains with 10 years of tasks"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "POC validated semantic zoom concept. Production app: apps/canvas"
    },
    {
      "id": "F002",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Pan navigation with drag",
      "steps": [
        "Click and drag on canvas to pan",
        "Cursor changes to grabbing while dragging",
        "Pan works in all directions (X and Y)",
        "Sidebar and timeline header sync with pan position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Implemented with mousedown/mousemove/mouseup events"
    },
    {
      "id": "F003",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom navigation with scroll wheel",
      "steps": [
        "Scroll up to zoom in, scroll down to zoom out",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor position",
        "Zoom respects MIN_ZOOM (0.1x) and MAX_ZOOM (5x) limits",
        "Zoom level indicator updates in header"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Zoom toward mouse position for natural feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F004",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Double-click zoom",
      "steps": [
        "Double-click zooms in 2x at click location",
        "Works at any current zoom level (respects max)",
        "Animation is smooth (no jarring jump)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Quick zoom in gesture"
    },
    {
      "id": "F005",
      "category": "navigation",
      "description": "Zoom button controls",
      "steps": [
        "Plus button zooms in by 1.5x",
        "Minus button zooms out by 1.5x",
        "Reset button returns to zoom 1.0 and pan origin",
        "Buttons zoom toward center of viewport"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Alternative for users without scroll wheel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F006",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Macro layer renders at zoom < 0.5x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom out below 0.5x to trigger Macro view",
        "Heatmap displays with Yellow-Orange-Red gradient",
        "Cell density reflects task count per month/train",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MACRO' with yellow badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Strategic 10-20 year overview"
    },
    {
      "id": "F007",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Meso layer renders at zoom 0.5x-2x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom to 0.5x-2x range to trigger Meso view",
        "Task blocks render as colored rectangles (DNA strips)",
        "Alternating row backgrounds for readability",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MESO' with blue badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Tactical 2-5 year view with task blocks"
    },
    {
      "id": "F008",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "Micro layer renders at zoom > 2x",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom above 2x to trigger Micro view",
        "Task cards render with shadows and rounded corners",
        "Grid lines show days (vertical) and trains (horizontal)",
        "Task type labels visible when card is wide enough",
        "LOD indicator shows 'MICRO' with green badge"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F003",
      "notes": "Operational 4-8 week view with detailed cards"
    },
    {
      "id": "F009",
      "category": "lod",
      "description": "LOD transitions are smooth",
      "steps": [
        "Zoom from Macro to Meso - no flickering",
        "Zoom from Meso to Micro - no flickering",
        "State persists correctly across transitions",
        "Performance stays smooth during transition"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F006",
      "notes": "Critical for Google Earth-like UX feel"
    },
    {
      "id": "F010",
      "category": "ui",
      "description": "Sidebar displays train IDs synced with canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Train IDs visible in left sidebar",
        "IDs update as canvas pans vertically",
        "Row height matches canvas zoom level",
        "Only visible trains are rendered (virtual scroll)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F002",
      "notes": "Sidebar syncs with panY position"
    },
    {
      "id": "F011",
      "category": "ui",
      "description": "Timeline header displays dates synced with canvas",
      "steps": [
        "Timeline labels update as canvas pans horizontally",
        "Granularity changes based on zoom (years/months/weeks/days)",
        "Labels are readable and don't overlap",
        "Timeline syncs with panX position"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F002",
      "notes": "Adaptive granularity: years at low zoom, days at high zoom"
    },
    {
      "id": "F012",
      "category": "visual",
      "description": "Conflict indicators visible on tasks",
      "steps": [
        "Tasks with conflicts show red border/glow",
        "Conflicts visible in Meso layer (red stroke)",
        "Conflicts visible in Micro layer (red glow shadow)",
        "~5% of tasks have conflict flag for testing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F007",
      "notes": "Red highlight draws attention to scheduling issues"
    },
    {
      "id": "F013",
      "category": "visual",
      "description": "Task colors match design specifications",
      "steps": [
        "Maintenance tasks: Blue gradient (#3b82f6 to #2563eb)",
        "Overhaul tasks: Orange gradient (#f59e0b to #d97706)",
        "Inspection tasks: Gray gradient (#9ca3af to #6b7280)",
        "Gradients render correctly at all zoom levels"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F007",
      "notes": "Color coding matches docs/UX_SPECIFICATION.md"
    },
    {
      "id": "F014",
      "category": "ux",
      "description": "Onboarding instructions display and auto-hide",
      "steps": [
        "Instructions tooltip visible on page load",
        "Shows: 'Scroll to zoom - Drag to pan - Double-click to zoom in'",
        "Auto-fades after 5 seconds",
        "Non-intrusive (pointer-events: none)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Progressive disclosure - teach on first use"
    },
    {
      "id": "F015",
      "category": "ux",
      "description": "LOD legend explains zoom levels",
      "steps": [
        "Legend panel visible in bottom-left corner",
        "Shows Macro (yellow), Meso (blue), Micro (green) colors",
        "Describes zoom thresholds (<0.5x, 0.5-2x, >2x)",
        "Helps users understand semantic zoom concept"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F001",
      "notes": "Educational UI element"
    },
    {
      "id": "F101",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Create Vite + React 18 + BlueprintJS app skeleton",
      "steps": [
        "Create apps/canvas directory with Vite config",
        "Install React 18.3.1, BlueprintJS 6.1.0",
        "Create basic App.tsx with BlueprintJS Navbar",
        "App runs on localhost:5173 without errors",
        "BlueprintJS styles load correctly"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": null,
      "notes": "Foundation for production app - React 18 required for BlueprintJS 6"
    },
    {
      "id": "F102",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Add R3F Canvas with OrthographicCamera",
      "steps": [
        "Install @react-three/fiber and @react-three/drei",
        "Create InfiniteCanvas component with R3F Canvas",
        "Setup OrthographicCamera for 2D-like view",
        "Canvas fills available space below navbar",
        "No WebGL errors in console"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F101",
      "notes": "R3F 8.x compatible with React 18. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-15."
    },
    {
      "id": "F103",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Basic BlueprintJS layout (Navbar, sidebar placeholder)",
      "steps": [
        "Navbar with app title and zoom indicator",
        "Left sidebar container for train IDs",
        "Main area for R3F canvas",
        "Layout uses flexbox, fills viewport",
        "Responsive to window resize"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "BlueprintJS Card, Navbar, ButtonGroup. Sidebar syncs with canvas panY. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-15."
    },
    {
      "id": "F104",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Data types and mock generator",
      "steps": [
        "Create Train, Task, Config TypeScript interfaces",
        "Implement generateData() function",
        "Generate 50 trains with 10 years of tasks",
        "~5% tasks have conflict flag",
        "Data available via React context or store"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F101",
      "notes": "types.ts + generator.ts in apps/canvas/src/data/. Persistent types use ISO strings, render types use numeric offsets."
    },
    {
      "id": "F105",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MesoLayer - InstancedMesh task blocks",
      "steps": [
        "Create MesoLayer component using InstancedMesh",
        "Render task blocks as colored rectangles",
        "Colors: blue (maintenance), orange (overhaul), gray (inspection)",
        "Alternating row backgrounds for readability",
        "Tasks visible at default zoom (1.0x)"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F104",
      "notes": "Implemented with InstancedMesh. Fixed architectural issue: camera init now uses R3F size (not container div). Tasks aligned with sidebar. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F106",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "Pan and Zoom camera controls",
      "steps": [
        "Drag to pan (mousedown + mousemove)",
        "Scroll to zoom (wheel event)",
        "Zoom centers on mouse cursor",
        "Respect MIN_ZOOM (0.1) and MAX_ZOOM (5.0)",
        "Cursor changes to grab/grabbing"
      ],
      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "Pan bounded to world limits. Zoom via wheel and buttons. Camera initialized using R3F size (not container div). Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F107",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "LOD state management",
      "steps": [
        "Create useViewport hook with zoom, panX, panY state",

        "LOD changes trigger layer visibility",
        "State persists across renders",
        "Export state for UI components"

      "passes": true,
      "blockedBy": "F102",
      "notes": "Implemented in App.tsx (getLOD, zoom/panY state). Hook extraction optional. Layer visibility tested when F105+ exist."
    },
    {
      "id": "F108",
      "category": "canvas",
      "description": "MacroLayer - Heatmap cells",

      "notes": "Implemented with InstancedMesh. Task density aggregated by month. Color gradient: light yellow -> amber -> orange -> red. Year markers included. Verified via Playwright MCP 2025-12-16."
    },
    {
      "id": "F109",
      "category": "canvas",

   162→## Session: 2025-12-16 (Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅
   163→
   164→### Performance Issues Resolved
   165→
   166→**Problems Fixed:**
   167→1. ✅ Zoom lagging/slow at high zoom - fixed with frustum culling
   168→2. ✅ Labels only appearing after seconds - fixed with lazy rendering (max 50 labels)
   169→3. ✅ White space around graph at minimum zoom - fixed with dynamic MIN_ZOOM
   170→4. ✅ Sidebar train list scrambled at small zoom - fixed by limiting zoom to ~0.57x
   171→
   172→**Optimizations Implemented:**
   173→
   174→#### 1. Frustum Culling (MicroLayer.tsx)
   175→- Added `filterVisibleTasks()` function to only render tasks within viewport bounds
   176→- Added 100px padding for smooth panning
   177→- Reduced rendering from ~5,500 tasks to only visible ones (~50-200)
   178→
   179→#### 2. InstancedMesh for Grid Lines (MicroLayer.tsx)
   180→- Converted 251 individual meshes to 2 InstancedMesh (horizontal + vertical lines)
   181→- Reduced draw calls from 251 to 2
   182→- Grid lines also use frustum culling based on viewport
   183→
   184→#### 3. InstancedMesh for Row Backgrounds (MicroLayer.tsx)
   185→- Converted 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh
   186→- Reduced draw calls from 50 to 1
   187→- Only renders visible rows based on viewport
   188→
   189→#### 4. Lazy Label Rendering (MicroLayer.tsx)
   190→- Limited HTML labels to max 50 (sorted by task width, largest first)
   191→- Labels only render when opacity > 0.3 (performance optimization)
   192→- Labels only for visible tasks within viewport
   193→
   194→#### 5. Dynamic MIN_ZOOM (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + App.tsx)
   195→- Added `effectiveMinZoom` calculation based on canvas size and world bounds
   196→- Formula: `minZoom = max(canvasWidth/worldWidth, canvasHeight/worldHeight)`
   197→- Added `onMinZoomChange` callback to sync with App.tsx
   198→- Now zoom stops at ~0.57x instead of 0.10x, preventing white space
   199→

   202→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx` (complete rewrite with optimizations)
   203→- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (minZoom state, handleMinZoomChange)

   379→## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)
   380→
   381→### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar
   382→
   383→**Symptoms:**
   384→- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
   385→- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
   386→- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)
   387→
   388→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   389→- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
   390→- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
   391→- Container div measured: 800x600
   392→- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
   393→- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
   394→- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070
   395→
   396→**Solution:**
   397→- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
   398→- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
   399→- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
   400→- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations
   401→
   402→**Files Changed:**
   403→- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
   404→  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
   405→  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
   406→  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
   407→  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
   408→- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
   409→  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
   410→  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds
   411→
   412→**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
   413→- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
   414→- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
   415→- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
   416→- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
   417→- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
   418→- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
   419→- ✅ No console errors
   420→
   421→**Key Insight:**
   422→When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).
   423→
   424→**Features Updated:**
   425→- F105: ✅ Passes (tasks aligned with sidebar)
   426→- F106: ✅ Passes (pan/zoom with correct bounds)
   427→- F114: ✅ Passes (conflict indicators already working)
   428→
   429→**Current State:**
   430→- F101-F107: ✅ Foundation + MesoLayer + Pan/Zoom complete
   431→- F108-F110: ❌ Pending (MacroLayer, MicroLayer, transitions)
   432→- F111-F114: ✅ Complete (sidebar, timeline partial, controls, conflicts)
   433→- F115-F117: ❌ Pending (polish)

   684→## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Self-Awareness System)
   685→
   686→### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness
   687→
   688→**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.
   689→
   690→**Root Cause Analysis:**
   691→1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
   692→2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
   693→3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
   694→4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand
   695→
   696→**Solution Implemented:**
   697→
   698→1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
   699→   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
   700→   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
   701→   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
   702→     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
   703→     - Analyze why local docs failed
   704→     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
   705→     - Document in progress.txt
   706→
   707→2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
   708→   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
   709→   - Added official Anthropic link
   710→   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
   711→   - Removed duplicate section from line 293
   712→
   713→**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.
   714→
   715→**Sources:**
   716→- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
   717→- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

   836→## Session: 2025-12-15 (Claude Code Hooks)
   837→
   838→### Implemented Harness Enforcement via Hooks
   839→
   840→Based on:
   841→- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
   842→- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)
   843→
   844→**Hooks Created:**
   845→- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
   846→- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
   847→- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
   848→- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
   849→- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn
   850→
   851→**Architecture:**
   852→```
   853→PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
   854→PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
   855→Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
   856→```

# Metro Integrated Planning System - Progress Log

## Session: 2025-12-16 (Harness Restructure) - COMPLETE ✅

### Harness Restructure: Priority Queue Implementation

**Problem Identified:**
- Priority tasks (HARNESS-001, UX-AUDIT-001, F118-F121) were buried in `archive.deferredFeatures`
- No clear execution order for pending features
- Missing QA testing protocol planning task

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Moved priority features to active list (top of features array):**
   - HARNESS-001 (priority 0): Configure Playwright headless
   - QA-PLAN-001 (priority 0, NEW): Plan QA testing protocol with deep thinking
   - UX-AUDIT-001 (priority 1): UX Best Practices Research
   - F118 (priority 2): Momentum/inertia panning
   - F119 (priority 2): Smooth zoom UX
   - F120 (priority 2): Double-click zoom (BUG FIX)
   - F121 (priority 2): Cross-platform scroll sensitivity
   - F116 (priority 3): Performance optimization
   - F117 (priority 4): Visual parity

2. **Added `priorityQueue` section to features.json:**
   - Priority 0 = immediate execution
   - Priority 1 = high priority
   - Priority 2+ = after dependencies

3. **Added `testingProtocol` section:**
   - Defines mandatory sequence: VISUAL TEST → CONSOLE CHECK → QA TEST → MARK COMPLETE
   - QA protocol to be defined in QA-PLAN-001

4. **Added QA-PLAN-001 task:**
   - Planning task using deep thinking mode
   - Design protocol to enforce QA tests AFTER visual tests
   - Based on UX-AUDIT-001 discoveries
   - Output: docs/QA_PROTOCOL.md + harness hooks

**Files Changed:**
- `features.json` (restructured with priority queue)
- `claude-progress.txt` (this update)

**Current Execution Order:**
```
1. HARNESS-001: Playwright headless (blockedBy: null)
2. QA-PLAN-001: QA protocol planning (blockedBy: HARNESS-001)
3. UX-AUDIT-001: UX research (blockedBy: HARNESS-001)
4. F118-F121: UX enhancements (blockedBy: UX-AUDIT-001)
5. F116: Performance (blockedBy: F110, can parallel with UX)
6. F117: Visual parity (blockedBy: F116)
```

**Next:** Execute HARNESS-001 (Configure Playwright headless)

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F115 - Instructions Toaster) - COMPLETE ✅

### F115: Instructions Toaster with auto-dismiss - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**
- Added BlueprintJS OverlayToaster to App.tsx
- Shows instruction toast on first load: "🖱️ Scroll to zoom • Drag to pan • Double-click to zoom in"
- Auto-dismisses after 5 seconds
- Uses Intent.PRIMARY styling with info-sign icon

**Technical Details:**
- BlueprintJS 6.x uses `OverlayToaster.createAsync()` (async API)
- Toast is non-blocking (uses React Portal)
- Cleanup on unmount via `toaster.clear()`

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (added toaster useEffect)
- `features.json` (F115 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Toast appears in accessibility tree with correct message
- ✅ Auto-dismisses after 5 seconds
- ✅ No console errors

**Commit:** `b4683fa`

**Current State:**
- F101-F115: ✅ Complete
- F116: ❌ Pending (Performance optimization)
- F117: ❌ Pending (Visual parity)

**Deferred UX Tasks (in priority order):**

| Priority | ID | Description |
|----------|-----|-------------|
| 0 | HARNESS-001 | Playwright headless without focus stealing (EXECUTE FIRST) |
| 1 | UX-AUDIT-001 | Deep UX Research (planning mode + deep thinking) |
| 2 | F118 | Momentum/inertia panning |
| 2 | F119 | Smooth zoom UX |
| 2 | F120 | Double-click zoom (bug) |
| 2 | F121 | Cross-platform scroll sensitivity |

**UX-AUDIT-001 Scope:**
- Visual elements: Canva, Figma, Google Maps, iOS, Android patterns

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F112 - Timeline Header) - COMPLETE ✅

### F112: Timeline header with adaptive granularity - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**
- Created `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` component
- Adaptive granularity based on zoom level:
  - < 0.5x: Years
  - 0.5-1.5x: Months (e.g., "Jan 2024", "Feb 2024")
  - 1.5-3.0x: Weeks (e.g., "Jan 7", "Jan 14")
  - > 3.0x: Days (e.g., "Mon, Jan 1", "Tue, Jan 2")
- Updated InfiniteCanvas to export `ViewportInfo` with `leftX`, `rightX`, `topY`
- Labels sync with horizontal pan position
- Gradient overlays fade labels at edges
- Tick marks below each label

**Technical Details:**
- Uses `pxPerDay` constant (10px) to calculate date positions
- Generates labels dynamically based on visible viewport range
- Date formatting uses browser's `toLocaleDateString()` API

**Bug Fixed:**
- Vite "does not provide an export named" error with TypeScript interfaces
- Root cause: esbuild removes type exports during dev mode transformation
- Solution: Use `import type { ViewportInfo }` instead of `import { ViewportInfo }`
- Reference: https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/2117

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/components/TimelineHeader.tsx` (NEW)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (ViewportInfo export, viewport X reporting)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (import TimelineHeader, viewport state)
- `features.json` (F112 passes: true)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Months at 1.0x zoom: "Jan 2024", "Feb 2024", "Mar 2024"
- ✅ Weeks at 1.5x zoom: "Jan 7", "Jan 14", "Jan 21"
- ✅ Days at 3.38x zoom: "Mon, Jan 1", "Tue, Jan 2", "Wed, Jan 3"
- ✅ Labels sync with horizontal pan
- ✅ No console errors

**Commit:** `735efce`

**Current State:**
- F101-F111, F113-F114: ✅ Complete
- F112: ✅ Complete (Timeline header)
- F115: ❌ Pending (Instructions Toaster)
- F116-F117: ❌ Pending (Performance, visual parity)

**Next:** F115 (Instructions Toaster) or continue with polish features

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (Performance Optimization) - COMPLETE ✅

### Performance Issues Resolved

**Problems Fixed:**
1. ✅ Zoom lagging/slow at high zoom - fixed with frustum culling
2. ✅ Labels only appearing after seconds - fixed with lazy rendering (max 50 labels)
3. ✅ White space around graph at minimum zoom - fixed with dynamic MIN_ZOOM
4. ✅ Sidebar train list scrambled at small zoom - fixed by limiting zoom to ~0.57x

**Optimizations Implemented:**

#### 1. Frustum Culling (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Added `filterVisibleTasks()` function to only render tasks within viewport bounds
- Added 100px padding for smooth panning
- Reduced rendering from ~5,500 tasks to only visible ones (~50-200)

#### 2. InstancedMesh for Grid Lines (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Converted 251 individual meshes to 2 InstancedMesh (horizontal + vertical lines)
- Reduced draw calls from 251 to 2
- Grid lines also use frustum culling based on viewport

#### 3. InstancedMesh for Row Backgrounds (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Converted 50 individual meshes to 1 InstancedMesh
- Reduced draw calls from 50 to 1
- Only renders visible rows based on viewport

#### 4. Lazy Label Rendering (MicroLayer.tsx)
- Limited HTML labels to max 50 (sorted by task width, largest first)
- Labels only render when opacity > 0.3 (performance optimization)
- Labels only for visible tasks within viewport

#### 5. Dynamic MIN_ZOOM (InfiniteCanvas.tsx + App.tsx)
- Added `effectiveMinZoom` calculation based on canvas size and world bounds
- Formula: `minZoom = max(canvasWidth/worldWidth, canvasHeight/worldHeight)`
- Added `onMinZoomChange` callback to sync with App.tsx
- Now zoom stops at ~0.57x instead of 0.10x, preventing white space

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (ViewportBounds, effectiveMinZoom, onMinZoomChange)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx` (complete rewrite with optimizations)
- `apps/canvas/src/App.tsx` (minZoom state, handleMinZoomChange)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MESO view at 1.0x - task blocks render correctly
- ✅ MICRO view at 2.25x - ~50 labels visible (not 2000), grid lines visible
- ✅ MACRO view at 0.44x - heatmap renders correctly
- ✅ Dynamic MIN_ZOOM at 0.57x - content fills viewport, no excessive white space
- ✅ Sidebar readable at minimum zoom (not scrambled)
- ✅ No console errors

**Performance Impact:**
| Metric | Before | After | Improvement |
|--------|--------|-------|-------------|
| Grid Lines Draw Calls | 251 | 2 | 99.2% reduction |
| Row Background Draw Calls | 50 | 1 | 98% reduction |
| HTML Labels (MICRO) | ~2000 | max 50 | 97.5% reduction |
| Tasks Rendered | ~5500 all | ~50-200 visible | 96-99% reduction |
| MIN_ZOOM | 0.10x (white space) | 0.57x (fills viewport) | UX improvement |

**Current State:**
- F101-F110: ✅ Complete (All 3 LOD layers with smooth transitions)
- F111: ✅ Complete (Sidebar sync)
- F112: ❌ Pending (Timeline header)
- F115-F117: ❌ Pending (Polish)
- Performance: ✅ Optimized for 60fps target

**Next:** Commit performance optimizations, then continue with F112 or F115

---

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F110 - Smooth LOD Transitions)

### F110: Smooth LOD transitions - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**
- Blend zones (±0.15) around LOD thresholds for smooth crossfade
- `calculateLayerOpacities()` function to compute opacity for each layer based on zoom
- `LODLayers` component to orchestrate layer rendering with opacity
- All three layers (Macro/Meso/Micro) now accept `opacity` prop

**Technical Details:**
- **LOD Thresholds:** MACRO < 0.5x, MESO 0.5-2x, MICRO > 2x
- **Blend Zone:** ±0.15 around each threshold
  - MACRO → MESO: blend zone 0.35-0.65
  - MESO → MICRO: blend zone 1.85-2.15
- During blend zones, both adjacent layers render simultaneously with interpolated opacity
- All Three.js materials use `transparent={opacity < 1}` for proper blending
- TaskLabels skip rendering when opacity < 0.3 (performance optimization)

**Components Updated:**
- `InfiniteCanvas.tsx`: Added LOD transition logic, LODLayers component
- `MacroLayer.tsx`: Added opacity prop to component and instancedMesh material
- `MesoLayer.tsx`: Added opacity prop to all subcomponents (RowBackgrounds, TaskBlocks, ConflictGlows)
- `MicroLayer.tsx`: Added opacity prop to all subcomponents (TaskCards, ConflictGlows, TaskLabels, GridLines, RowBackgrounds)

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ MESO view at 1.0x - task blocks render correctly
- ✅ MACRO view at 0.44x - heatmap visible, crossfade shows both MACRO and MESO layers simultaneously during transition
- ✅ MICRO view at 2.25x - task cards with labels, grid lines visible
- ✅ No visual artifacts during zoom transitions
- ✅ No console errors

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` (LOD transition system)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MacroLayer.tsx` (opacity support)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MesoLayer.tsx` (opacity support)
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx` (opacity support)
- `features.json` (F110 passes: true)

**Commit:** `553a40c`

- F101-F110: ✅ All 3 LOD layers with smooth transitions
- F111: ✅ Sidebar sync (already complete)

- F115-F117: ❌ Pending (Polish)

**Next:** F112 (Timeline header) or F115 (Instructions Toaster)

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F109 - MicroLayer Task Cards)

### F109: MicroLayer - Html task cards with labels - COMPLETE

**Implemented:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/layers/MicroLayer.tsx` - New component for detailed view at zoom > 2x
- Task cards using InstancedMesh (same pattern as MesoLayer for consistency)
- HTML labels via drei Html component on wider tasks
- Grid lines (week boundaries, train rows)
- Alternating row backgrounds for readability
- Conflict glow effects behind tasks with hasConflict flag

**Technical Details:**
- Uses Three.js InstancedMesh for task cards (single draw call)
- drei Html only for text labels (avoids scaling issues)
- Color mapping: blue (maintenance), orange (overhaul), gray (inspection)
- Red color for conflict tasks with glow effect
- Labels show task type (Maintenance/Overhaul/Inspection) when card is wide enough

**Initial Approach (Failed):**
- First tried using drei Html for entire task cards
- Cards appeared as tiny dots due to Html rendering at screen-space size
- Even with scale prop adjustments, couldn't get proper sizing

**Final Solution:**
- Switched to InstancedMesh for task card geometry (consistent with MesoLayer)
- Use Html only for text labels overlaid on wider tasks

## Session: 2025-12-16 (F105/F106 Architectural Fix - Camera Alignment)

### Problem: Tasks Not Aligned with Sidebar

**Symptoms:**
- Tasks appeared in center-right of canvas instead of left edge
- Tasks didn't align with train rows in sidebar
- Camera could be dragged infinitely (no bounds)

**Root Cause Analysis:**
- InfiniteCanvas used `containerRef.getBoundingClientRect()` to measure canvas size
- R3F's actual canvas size (from `useThree().size`) was different due to flexbox layout
- Container div measured: 800x600
- Actual R3F canvas: 1070x679
- Camera was positioned incorrectly: at (400, -300) instead of (535, -339.5)
- This caused viewport to show X from -135 to 935 instead of 0 to 1070

**Solution:**
- Created `CameraController` component that runs INSIDE the R3F Canvas context
- Uses `useThree().size` to get actual canvas dimensions
- Initializes camera position correctly on first render
- Reports actual size back to parent for pan/zoom calculations

**Files Changed:**
- `apps/canvas/src/canvas/InfiniteCanvas.tsx` - Complete refactor
  - Removed old `CameraSync` component
  - Added new `CameraController` with proper R3F size handling
  - Camera initialization now happens inside R3F context
  - Pan/zoom handlers use correct canvas dimensions
- `apps/canvas/src/data/types.ts` - Added helper functions
  - `calculateDataBounds()` for computing task extent
  - `calculateFitToView()` for D3.js-style fit-to-bounds

**Visual Tests (Playwright MCP):**
- ✅ Tasks aligned with train rows (TRAIN-001 = first task row)
- ✅ Tasks start near left edge of canvas
- ✅ Pan bounded to world limits (can't drag infinitely)
- ✅ Zoom works correctly (+/- buttons, wheel)
- ✅ Reset View returns to initial state
- ✅ Conflict indicators visible (red tasks)
- ✅ No console errors

**Key Insight:**
When using R3F, always get canvas dimensions from `useThree().size`, not from the container div. The container and canvas can have different sizes due to CSS layout (flexbox, padding, borders).

**Features Updated:**
- F105: ✅ Passes (tasks aligned with sidebar)
- F106: ✅ Passes (pan/zoom with correct bounds)
- F114: ✅ Passes (conflict indicators already working)

**Current State:**
- F101-F107: ✅ Foundation + MesoLayer + Pan/Zoom complete
- F108-F110: ❌ Pending (MacroLayer, MicroLayer, transitions)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Self-Awareness System)

### Meta-Reflection: Why Claude Didn't Understand the Harness

**Problem:** Claude Code was starting sessions without understanding what "Harness" is, despite existing documentation.

**Root Cause Analysis:**
1. **Poor positioning:** Harness section was at line 293 of CLAUDE.md (after 200+ lines of other content)
2. **Hook assumed prior knowledge:** `session-start.sh` said "what to do" but not "what is" or "why"
3. **No research fallback:** No links to official Anthropic documentation
4. **No self-correction mechanism:** No instruction for Claude to fix the harness if it doesn't understand

**Solution Implemented:**

1. **Updated `session-start.sh`:**
   - Added "WHAT IS A HARNESS?" section with definition
   - Added "WORK PROTOCOL" section with steps
   - Added "SELF-CORRECTION PROTOCOL" section with instructions to:
     - Search Anthropic docs if not understood
     - Analyze why local docs failed
     - Fix CLAUDE.md or hooks
     - Document in progress.txt

2. **Updated `CLAUDE.md`:**
   - Moved harness section to BEGINNING (right after Project Overview)
   - Added official Anthropic link
   - Added Self-Correction Protocol
   - Removed duplicate section from line 293

**Key Innovation:** Self-correction loop where each session leaves the harness clearer for the next.

**Sources:**
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Claude Code Hooks)

### Implemented Harness Enforcement via Hooks

Based on:
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Claude Code Hooks Guide](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/hooks-guide)

**Hooks Created:**
- [x] `session-start.sh` (SessionStart) - Harness protocol reminders + package versions
- [x] `protect-package-json.sh` (PreToolUse) - BLOCKS manual package.json edits
- [x] `after-write.sh` (PostToolUse) - Quick feedback after file edits
- [x] `task-type-detector.py` (UserPromptSubmit) - Task-type aware reminders
- [x] `end-of-turn-check.sh` (Stop) - Quality gate at end of turn

**Architecture:**
```
PreToolUse  → BLOCKING (prevent bad actions)
PostToolUse → FAST (quick feedback)
Stop        → QUALITY GATE (typecheck, visual test reminders)
```

1) Domain exports: re-export Train, Task, Conflict, MockDataset from packages/domain/src/index.ts and update gl-engine imports to '@mms/domain'
2) Fix usePlanning typing: verify toolkit MachineHook generics; ensure slice keys are inferred (no string literal widening). If needed, declare `const key = 'viewport' as const` before calling usePlanning(key)
3) Confirm GLSL raw types present (packages/gl-engine/src/types/import-raw.d.ts)
4) Run `bun run typecheck` until all packages pass; only then proceed to UI work

How to resume
- ./init.sh --no-server
- bun run typecheck
- bun run dev (or cd apps/system && bun run dev)

---

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Vite migration)

### Summary
- Migrated @mms/domain and @mms/gl-engine to Vite build (like @mms/toolkit)
- Created vite.config.ts, tsconfig.prod.json for both packages
- Fixed imports to use `@mms/domain` instead of `@mms/domain/src/...`
- Replaced usePlanning() with usePlanningSelector() + PlanningActions
- Added @types/react to app-system
- TypeScript typecheck now passes across all packages

### State
- `bun run typecheck` passes (4 packages)
- `bun run build` fails on app-system due to Tailwind 4.0 beta issue
- Dev mode should work: `bun run dev`

### Files changed
- packages/domain/package.json, tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.prod.json
- packages/gl-engine/package.json, tsconfig.json, vite.config.ts, tsconfig.prod.json
- packages/gl-engine/src/state/world-machine.ts
- packages/gl-engine/src/camera/CameraRig.tsx
- packages/gl-engine/src/layers/MacroLayer.tsx, MesoLayer.tsx, MicroLayer.tsx
- apps/system/package.json, src/components/TrainList.tsx, TimeScrubber.tsx
- apps/system/src/pages/index.astro

---

## Recurring Issues & Solutions

### Issue: R3F or Three.js import errors
**Solution**: Use `bun add` to install packages, never edit package.json manually

### Issue: Astro hydration issues with React
**Solution**: Always use `client:only="react"` for R3F components

### Issue: Shader compilation errors
**Solution**: Check GLSL syntax, verify uniform names match

### Issue: TypeScript errors with toolkit state machine
**Solution**: Use usePlanningSelector() instead of usePlanning() for type safety

---

## Session: 2025-12-15 (Harness Integrity Verification)

### Summary

Comprehensive audit and cleanup of the project harness system based on Anthropic's harness protocol.

**Key Understanding:**
- CLAUDE.md is the **AUTHORITATIVE** document (defines stack and rules)
- features.json is the **SOURCE OF TRUTH** (feature status)
- claude-progress.txt is the **SESSION LOG** (history)
- All other files must **ALIGN** with these three

### Files DELETED (obsolete/incompatible)

| File/Directory | Reason |
|----------------|--------|
| `INITIAL_PROMPT.md` | Superseded requirements (React 19, Astro, Tailwind) |
| `PROMPT.md` | Portuguese, obsolete pnpm commands |
| `apps/system/` | Legacy Astro + React 19 app (incompatible with React 18 stack) |
| `packages/gl-engine/` | Required React 19 (R3F 9.x), incompatible with apps/canvas |
| `packages/ui/` | Required React 19, incompatible |
| `packages/domain/` | Not part of current stack (apps/canvas has own types) |

### Files UPDATED (aligned with CLAUDE.md)

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `README.md` (root) | Tech stack: React 18.3.1, Vite 5.x, BlueprintJS 6.1.0; Project structure updated |
| `CONTINUE.md` | F016 → F102 (next feature reference) |
| `CLAUDE_NOTES.md` | Removed Astro/React 19/Tailwind references; Updated apps/system → apps/canvas |
| `design/README.md` | Paths updated from packages/gl-engine → apps/canvas; Updated checklist |
| `packages/toolkit/README.md` | @belt/toolkit → @mms/toolkit; pnpm → bun |
| `apps/canvas/README.md` | Replaced generic Vite template with MMS-specific documentation |

### PENDING (requires manual action)

**Root package.json** needs script filter update (blocked by hook):
```json
// Current (broken):
"dev": "turbo dev --filter=@mms/app-system",
"preview": "bun --bun run --filter '@mms/app-system' preview",

// Should be:
"dev": "turbo dev --filter=@mms/canvas",
"preview": "bun --bun run --filter '@mms/canvas' preview",
```

### Harness State After Cleanup

**Production Stack (apps/canvas):**
- Vite 5.x
- React 18.3.1 (required for BlueprintJS 6)
- BlueprintJS 6.1.0
- @react-three/fiber 8.x (pairs with React 18)
- @react-three/drei 9.x
- three 0.170.x

**Feature Status:**
- F001-F015: POC features (passed, archived)
- F101: Production skeleton (passed)
- F102+: Pending implementation

**Shared Package:**
- @mms/toolkit (React 18 || 19 compatible)

### Commit

**Hash:** `995efa4`
**Message:** `chore: harness integrity verification and legacy cleanup`

**Files changed:** 60 files, +163 -1542 lines

### Next Steps

1. Run `bun run dev` to verify production app starts
2. Continue with F102 (R3F Canvas integration)

content.thinking:
Vejo que o projeto usa o Playwright MCP (mcp__playwright__*). Preciso pesquisar como configurar o Playwright para rodar em modo headless sem roubar foco.

Vou pesquisar a documentação do Playwright MCP para entender como configurá-lo.

content.text: Vou pesquisar a documentação do Playwright MCP para configurar o modo headless.

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Playwright MCP server headless configuration 2025"

Web search error: unavailable

I apologize, but the web search tool is currently unavailable. However, I can share some general information about Playwright MCP server configuration from my existing knowledge:

**Playwright MCP Server Overview:**
The Playwright MCP (Model Context Protocol) server allows AI assistants to control a browser through Playwright for web automation tasks.

**Common Headless Configuration Options:**

When configuring Playwright MCP server, you typically have options like:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "playwright": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@playwright/mcp@latest"],
      "env": {
        "PLAYWRIGHT_HEADLESS": "true"
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Or using command-line arguments:
```json
{
  "args": ["@playwright/mcp@latest", "--headless"]
}
```

**Key Configuration Points:**
- `--headless` or `--headless=true` - Run browser without visible UI
- `--browser=chromium|firefox|webkit` - Choose browser engine
- Environment variables can also control behavior

If you need the most up-to-date configuration options for 2025, I'd recommend checking:
1. The official Playwright MCP GitHub repository
2. Playwright's official documentation at playwright.dev
3. The npm package page for `@playwright/mcp`

Would you like me to try the search again, or can I help with specific configuration questions based on my existing knowledge?


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.thinking: A pesquisa web não está disponível. Vou tentar verificar diretamente a documentação oficial do Playwright MCP via WebFetch.

content.thinking:
Perfeito! Encontrei a informação necessária. O Playwright MCP roda em modo "headed" (com janela visível) por padrão. Para rodar em modo headless (sem janela visível), preciso adicionar `--headless` aos argumentos do MCP.

Vou verificar qual é a configuração atual do Playwright MCP no sistema global.

  "cachedChangelog": "# Changelog

## 2.0.70

- Added Enter key to accept and submit prompt suggestions immediately (tab still accepts for editing)
- Added wildcard syntax `mcp__server__*` for MCP tool permissions to allow or deny all tools from a server
- Added auto-update toggle for plugin marketplaces, allowing per-marketplace control over automatic updates
- Added `plan_mode_required` spawn parameter for teammates to require plan approval before implementing changes
- Added `current_usage` field to status line input, enabling accurate context window percentage calculations
- Fixed input being cleared when processing queued commands while the user was typing
- Fixed prompt suggestions replacing typed input when pressing Tab
- Fixed diff view not updating when terminal is resized
- Improved memory usage by 3x for large conversations
- Improved resolution of stats screenshots copied to clipboard (Ctrl+S) for crisper images
- Removed # shortcut for quick memory entry (tell Claude to edit your CLAUDE.md instead)
- Fix thinking mode toggle in /config not persisting correctly
- Improve UI for file creation permission dialog

## 2.0.69

- Minor bugfixes

## 2.0.68

- Fixed IME (Input Method Editor) support for languages like Chinese, Japanese, and Korean by correctly positioning the composition window at the cursor
- Fixed a bug where disallowed MCP tools were visible to the model
- Fixed an issue where steering messages could be lost while a subagent is working
- Fixed Option+Arrow word navigation treating entire CJK (Chinese, Japanese, Korean) text sequences as a single word instead of navigating by word boundaries
- Improved plan mode exit UX: show simplified yes/no dialog when exiting with empty or missing plan instead of throwing an error
- Add support for enterprise managed settings. Contact your Anthropic account team to enable this feature.

## 2.0.67

- Thinking mode is now enabled by default for Opus 4.5
- Thinking mode configuration has moved to /config
- Added search functionality to `/permissions` command with `/` keyboard shortcut for filtering rules by tool name
- Show reason why autoupdater is disabled in `/doctor`
- Fixed false "Another process is currently updating Claude" error when running `claude update` while another instance is already on the latest version
- Fixed MCP servers from `.mcp.json` being stuck in pending state when running in non-interactive mode (`-p` flag or piped input)
- Fixed scroll position resetting after deleting a permission rule in `/permissions`
- Fixed word deletion (opt+delete) and word navigation (opt+arrow) not working correctly with non-Latin text such as Cyrillic, Greek, Arabic, Hebrew, Thai, and Chinese
- Fixed `claude install --force` not bypassing stale lock files
- Fixed consecutive @~/ file references in CLAUDE.md being incorrectly parsed due to markdown strikethrough interference
- Windows: Fixed plugin MCP servers failing due to colons in log directory paths

## 2.0.65

- Added ability to switch models while writing a prompt using alt+p (linux, windows), option+p (macos).
- Added context window information to status line input
- Added `fileSuggestion` setting for custom `@` file search commands
- Added `CLAUDE_CODE_SHELL` environment variable to override automatic shell detection (useful when login shell differs from actual working shell)
- Fixed prompt not being saved to history when aborting a query with Escape
- Fixed Read tool image handling to identify format from bytes instead of file extension

## 2.0.64

- Made auto-compacting instant
- Agents and bash commands can run asynchronously and send messages to wake up the main agent
- /stats now provides users with interesting CC stats, such as favorite model, usage graph, usage streak
- Added named session support: use `/rename` to name sessions, `/resume <name>` in REPL or `claude --resume <name>` from the terminal to resume them
- Added support for .claude/rules/`.  See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/memory for details.
- Added image dimension metadata when images are resized, enabling accurate coordinate mappings for large images
- Fixed auto-loading .env when using native installer
- Fixed `--system-prompt` being ignored when using `--continue` or `--resume` flags
- Improved `/resume` screen with grouped forked sessions and keyboard shortcuts for preview (P) and rename (R)
- VSCode: Added copy-to-clipboard button on code blocks and bash tool inputs
- VSCode: Fixed extension not working on Windows ARM64 by falling back to x64 binary via emulation
- Bedrock: Improve efficiency of token counting
- Bedrock: Add support for `aws login` AWS Management Console credentials
- Unshipped AgentOutputTool and BashOutputTool, in favor of a new unified TaskOutputTool

## 2.0.62

- Added "(Recommended)" indicator for multiple-choice questions, with the recommended option moved to the top of the list
- Added `attribution` setting to customize commit and PR bylines (deprecates `includeCoAuthoredBy`)
- Fixed duplicate slash commands appearing when ~/.claude is symlinked to a project directory
- Fixed slash command selection not working when multiple commands share the same name
- Fixed an issue where skill files inside symlinked skill directories could become circular symlinks
- Fixed running versions getting removed because lock file incorrectly going stale
- Fixed IDE diff tab not closing when rejecting file changes

## 2.0.61

- Reverted VSCode support for multiple terminal clients due to responsiveness issues.

## 2.0.60

- Added background agent support. Agents run in the background while you work
- Added --disable-slash-commands CLI flag to disable all slash commands
- Added model name to "Co-Authored-By" commit messages
- Enabled "/mcp enable [server-name]" or "/mcp disable [server-name]" to quickly toggle all servers
- Updated Fetch to skip summarization for pre-approved websites
- VSCode: Added support for multiple terminal clients connecting to the IDE server simultaneously

## 2.0.59

- Added --agent CLI flag to override the agent setting for the current session
- Added `agent` setting to configure main thread with a specific agent's system prompt, tool restrictions, and model
- VS Code: Fixed .claude.json config file being read from incorrect location

## 2.0.58

- Pro users now have access to Opus 4.5 as part of their subscription!
- Fixed timer duration showing "11m 60s" instead of "12m 0s"
- Windows: Managed settings now prefer `C:\Program Files\ClaudeCode` if it exists. Support for `C:\ProgramData\ClaudeCode` will be removed in a future version.

## 2.0.57

- Added feedback input when rejecting plans, allowing users to tell Claude what to change
- VSCode: Added streaming message support for real-time response display

## 2.0.56

- Added setting to enable/disable terminal progress bar (OSC 9;4)
- VSCode Extension: Added support for VS Code's secondary sidebar (VS Code 1.97+), allowing Claude Code to be displayed in the right sidebar while keeping the file explorer on the left. Requires setting sidebar as Preferred Location in the config.

## 2.0.55

- Fixed proxy DNS resolution being forced on by default. Now opt-in via `CLAUDE_CODE_PROXY_RESOLVES_HOSTS=true` environment variable
- Fixed keyboard navigation becoming unresponsive when holding down arrow keys in memory location selector
- Improved AskUserQuestion tool to auto-submit single-select questions on the last question, eliminating the extra review screen for simple question flows
- Improved fuzzy matching for `@` file suggestions with faster, more accurate results

## 2.0.54

- Hooks: Enable PermissionRequest hooks to process 'always allow' suggestions and apply permission updates
- Fix issue with excessive iTerm notifications

## 2.0.52

- Fixed duplicate message display when starting Claude with a command line argument
- Fixed `/usage` command progress bars to fill up as usage increases (instead of showing remaining percentage)
- Fixed image pasting not working on Linux systems running Wayland (now falls back to wl-paste when xclip is unavailable)
- Permit some uses of `$!` in bash commands

## 2.0.51

- Added Opus 4.5! https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-5
- Introducing Claude Code for Desktop: https://claude.com/download
- To give you room to try out our new model, we've updated usage limits for Claude Code users. See the Claude Opus 4.5 blog for full details
- Pro users can now purchase extra usage for access to Opus 4.5 in Claude Code
- Plan Mode now builds more precise plans and executes more thoroughly
- Usage limit notifications now easier to understand
- Switched `/usage` back to "% used"
- Fixed handling of thinking errors
- Fixed performance regression

## 2.0.50

- Fixed bug preventing calling MCP tools that have nested references in their input schemas
- Silenced a noisy but harmless error during upgrades
- Improved ultrathink text display
- Improved clarity of 5-hour session limit warning message

## 2.0.49

- Added readline-style ctrl-y for pasting deleted text
- Improved clarity of usage limit warning message
- Fixed handling of subagent permissions

## 2.0.47

- Improved error messages and validation for `claude --teleport`
- Improved error handling in `/usage`
- Fixed race condition with history entry not getting logged at exit
- Fixed Vertex AI configuration not being applied from `settings.json`

## 2.0.46

- Fixed image files being reported with incorrect media type when format cannot be detected from metadata

## 2.0.45

- Added support for Microsoft Foundry! See https://code.claude.com/docs/en/azure-ai-foundry
- Added `PermissionRequest` hook to automatically approve or deny tool permission requests with custom logic
- Send background tasks to Claude Code on the web by starting a message with `&`

## 2.0.43

- Added `permissionMode` field for custom agents
- Added `tool_use_id` field to `PreToolUseHookInput` and `PostToolUseHookInput` types
- Added skills frontmatter field to declare skills to auto-load for subagents
- Added the `SubagentStart` hook event
- Fixed nested `CLAUDE.md` files not loading when @-mentioning files
- Fixed duplicate rendering of some messages in the UI
- Fixed some visual flickers
- Fixed NotebookEdit tool inserting cells at incorrect positions when cell IDs matched the pattern `cell-N`

## 2.0.42

- Added `agent_id` and `agent_transcript_path` fields to `SubagentStop` hooks.

## 2.0.41

- Added `model` parameter to prompt-based stop hooks, allowing users to specify a custom model for hook evaluation
- Fixed slash commands from user settings being loaded twice, which could cause rendering issues
- Fixed incorrect labeling of user settings vs project settings in command descriptions
- Fixed crash when plugin command hooks timeout during execution
- Fixed: Bedrock users no longer see duplicate Opus entries in the /model picker when using `--model haiku`
- Fixed broken security documentation links in trust dialogs and onboarding
- Fixed issue where pressing ESC to close the diff modal would also interrupt the model
- ctrl-r history search landing on a slash command no longer cancels the search
- SDK: Support custom timeouts for hooks
- Allow more safe git commands to run without approval
- Plugins: Added support for sharing and installing output styles
- Teleporting a session from web will automatically set the upstream branch

## 2.0.37

- Fixed how idleness is computed for notifications
- Hooks: Added matcher values for Notification hook events
- Output Styles: Added `keep-coding-instructions` option to frontmatter

## 2.0.36

- Fixed: DISABLE_AUTOUPDATER environment variable now properly disables package manager update notifications
- Fixed queued messages being incorrectly executed as bash commands
- Fixed input being lost when typing while a queued message is processed

## 2.0.35

- Improve fuzzy search results when searching commands
- Improved VS Code extension to respect `chat.fontSize` and `chat.fontFamily` settings throughout the entire UI, and apply font changes immediately without requiring reload
- Added `CLAUDE_CODE_EXIT_AFTER_STOP_DELAY` environment variable to automatically exit SDK mode after a specified idle duration, useful for automated workflows and scripts
- Migrated `ignorePatterns` from project config to deny permissions in the localSettings.
- Fixed menu navigation getting stuck on items with empty string or other falsy values (e.g., in the `/hooks` menu)

## 2.0.34

- VSCode Extension: Added setting to configure the initial permission mode for new conversations
- Improved file path suggestion performance with native Rust-based fuzzy finder
- Fixed infinite token refresh loop that caused MCP servers with OAuth (e.g., Slack) to hang during connection
- Fixed memory crash when reading or writing large files (especially base64-encoded images)

## 2.0.33

- Native binary installs now launch quicker.
- Fixed `claude doctor` incorrectly detecting Homebrew vs npm-global installations by properly resolving symlinks
- Fixed `claude mcp serve` exposing tools with incompatible outputSchemas

## 2.0.32

- Un-deprecate output styles based on community feedback
- Added `companyAnnouncements` setting for displaying announcements on startup
- Fixed hook progress messages not updating correctly during PostToolUse hook execution

## 2.0.31

- Windows: native installation uses shift+tab as shortcut for mode switching, instead of alt+m
- Vertex: add support for Web Search on supported models
- VSCode: Adding the respectGitIgnore configuration to include .gitignored files in file searches (defaults to true)
- Fixed a bug with subagents and MCP servers related to "Tool names must be unique" error
- Fixed issue causing `/compact` to fail with `prompt_too_long` by making it respect existing compact boundaries
- Fixed plugin uninstall not removing plugins

## 2.0.30

- Added helpful hint to run `security unlock-keychain` when encountering API key errors on macOS with locked keychain
- Added `allowUnsandboxedCommands` sandbox setting to disable the dangerouslyDisableSandbox escape hatch at policy level
- Added `disallowedTools` field to custom agent definitions for explicit tool blocking
- Added prompt-based stop hooks
- VSCode: Added respectGitIgnore configuration to include .gitignored files in file searches (defaults to true)
- Enabled SSE MCP servers on native build
- Deprecated output styles. Review options in `/output-style` and use --system-prompt-file, --system-prompt, --append-system-prompt, CLAUDE.md, or plugins instead
- Removed support for custom ripgrep configuration, resolving an issue where Search returns no results and config discovery fails
- Fixed Explore agent creating unwanted .md investigation files during codebase exploration
- Fixed a bug where `/context` would sometimes fail with "max_tokens must be greater than thinking.budget_tokens" error message
- Fixed `--mcp-config` flag to correctly override file-based MCP configurations
- Fixed bug that saved session permissions to local settings
- Fixed MCP tools not being available to sub-agents
- Fixed hooks and plugins not executing when using --dangerously-skip-permissions flag
- Fixed delay when navigating through typeahead suggestions with arrow keys
- VSCode: Restored selection indicator in input footer showing current file or code selection status

## 2.0.28

- Plan mode: introduced new Plan subagent
- Subagents: claude can now choose to resume subagents
- Subagents: claude can dynamically choose the model used by its subagents
- SDK: added --max-budget-usd flag
- Discovery of custom slash commands, subagents, and output styles no longer respects .gitignore
- Stop `/terminal-setup` from adding backslash to `Shift + Enter` in VS Code
- Add branch and tag support for git-based plugins and marketplaces using fragment syntax (e.g., `owner/repo#branch`)
- Fixed a bug where macOS permission prompts would show up upon initial launch when launching from home directory
- Various other bug fixes

## 2.0.27

- New UI for permission prompts
- Added current branch filtering and search to session resume screen for easier navigation
- Fixed directory @-mention causing "No assistant message found" error
- VSCode Extension: Add config setting to include .gitignored files in file searches
- VSCode Extension: Bug fixes for unrelated 'Warmup' conversations, and configuration/settings occasionally being reset to defaults

## 2.0.25

- Removed legacy SDK entrypoint. Please migrate to @anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk for future SDK updates: https://platform.claude.com/docs/en/agent-sdk/migration-guide

## 2.0.24

- Fixed a bug where project-level skills were not loading when --setting-sources 'project' was specified
- Claude Code Web: Support for Web -> CLI teleport
- Sandbox: Releasing a sandbox mode for the BashTool on Linux & Mac
- Bedrock: Display awsAuthRefresh output when auth is required

## 2.0.22

- Fixed content layout shift when scrolling through slash commands
- IDE: Add toggle to enable/disable thinking.
- Fix bug causing duplicate permission prompts with parallel tool calls
- Add support for enterprise managed MCP allowlist and denylist

## 2.0.21

- Support MCP `structuredContent` field in tool responses
- Added an interactive question tool
- Claude will now ask you questions more often in plan mode
- Added Haiku 4.5 as a model option for Pro users
- Fixed an issue where queued commands don't have access to previous messages' output

## 2.0.20

- Added support for Claude Skills

## 2.0.19

- Auto-background long-running bash commands instead of killing them. Customize with BASH_DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS
- Fixed a bug where Haiku was unnecessarily called in print mode

## 2.0.17

- Added Haiku 4.5 to model selector!
- Haiku 4.5 automatically uses Sonnet in plan mode, and Haiku for execution (i.e. SonnetPlan by default)
- 3P (Bedrock and Vertex) are not automatically upgraded yet. Manual upgrading can be done through setting `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL`
- Introducing the Explore subagent. Powered by Haiku it'll search through your codebase efficiently to save context!
- OTEL: support HTTP_PROXY and HTTPS_PROXY
- `CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_NONESSENTIAL_TRAFFIC` now disables release notes fetching

## 2.0.15

- Fixed bug with resuming where previously created files needed to be read again before writing
- Fixed bug with `-p` mode where @-mentioned files needed to be read again before writing

## 2.0.14

- Fix @-mentioning MCP servers to toggle them on/off
- Improve permission checks for bash with inline env vars
- Fix ultrathink + thinking toggle
- Reduce unnecessary logins
- Document --system-prompt
- Several improvements to rendering
- Plugins UI polish

## 2.0.13

- Fixed `/plugin` not working on native build

## 2.0.12

- **Plugin System Released**: Extend Claude Code with custom commands, agents, hooks, and MCP servers from marketplaces
- `/plugin install`, `/plugin enable/disable`, `/plugin marketplace` commands for plugin management
- Repository-level plugin configuration via `extraKnownMarketplaces` for team collaboration
- `/plugin validate` command for validating plugin structure and configuration
- Plugin announcement blog post at https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-code-plugins
- Plugin documentation available at https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins
- Comprehensive error messages and diagnostics via `/doctor` command
- Avoid flickering in `/model` selector
- Improvements to `/help`
- Avoid mentioning hooks in `/resume` summaries
- Changes to the "verbose" setting in `/config` now persist across sessions

## 2.0.11

- Reduced system prompt size by 1.4k tokens
- IDE: Fixed keyboard shortcuts and focus issues for smoother interaction
- Fixed Opus fallback rate limit errors appearing incorrectly
- Fixed /add-dir command selecting wrong default tab

## 2.0.10

- Rewrote terminal renderer for buttery smooth UI
- Enable/disable MCP servers by @mentioning, or in /mcp
- Added tab completion for shell commands in bash mode
- PreToolUse hooks can now modify tool inputs
- Press Ctrl-G to edit your prompt in your system's configured text editor
- Fixes for bash permission checks with environment variables in the command

## 2.0.9

- Fix regression where bash backgrounding stopped working

## 2.0.8

- Update Bedrock default Sonnet model to `global.anthropic.claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929-v1:0`
- IDE: Add drag-and-drop support for files and folders in chat
- /context: Fix counting for thinking blocks
- Improve message rendering for users with light themes on dark terminals
- Remove deprecated .claude.json allowedTools, ignorePatterns, env, and todoFeatureEnabled config options (instead, configure these in your settings.json)

## 2.0.5

- IDE: Fix IME unintended message submission with Enter and Tab
- IDE: Add "Open in Terminal" link in login screen
- Fix unhandled OAuth expiration 401 API errors
- SDK: Added SDKUserMessageReplay.isReplay to prevent duplicate messages

## 2.0.1

- Skip Sonnet 4.5 default model setting change for Bedrock and Vertex
- Various bug fixes and presentation improvements

## 2.0.0

- New native VS Code extension
- Fresh coat of paint throughout the whole app
- /rewind a conversation to undo code changes
- /usage command to see plan limits
- Tab to toggle thinking (sticky across sessions)
- Ctrl-R to search history
- Unshipped claude config command
- Hooks: Reduced PostToolUse 'tool_use' ids were found without 'tool_result' blocks errors
- SDK: The Claude Code SDK is now the Claude Agent SDK
- Add subagents dynamically with `--agents` flag

## 1.0.126

- Enable /context command for Bedrock and Vertex
- Add mTLS support for HTTP-based OpenTelemetry exporters

## 1.0.124

- Set `CLAUDE_BASH_NO_LOGIN` environment variable to 1 or true to to skip login shell for BashTool
- Fix Bedrock and Vertex environment variables evaluating all strings as truthy
- No longer inform Claude of the list of allowed tools when permission is denied
- Fixed security vulnerability in Bash tool permission checks
- Improved VSCode extension performance for large files

## 1.0.123

- Bash permission rules now support output redirections when matching (e.g., `Bash(python:*)` matches `python script.py > output.txt`)
- Fixed thinking mode triggering on negation phrases like "don't think"
- Fixed rendering performance degradation during token streaming
- Added SlashCommand tool, which enables Claude to invoke your slash commands. https://code.claude.com/docs/en/slash-commands#SlashCommand-tool
- Enhanced BashTool environment snapshot logging
- Fixed a bug where resuming a conversation in headless mode would sometimes enable thinking unnecessarily
- Migrated --debug logging to a file, to enable easy tailing & filtering

## 1.0.120

- Fix input lag during typing, especially noticeable with large prompts
- Improved VSCode extension command registry and sessions dialog user experience
- Enhanced sessions dialog responsiveness and visual feedback
- Fixed IDE compatibility issue by removing worktree support check
- Fixed security vulnerability where Bash tool permission checks could be bypassed using prefix matching

## 1.0.119

- Fix Windows issue where process visually freezes on entering interactive mode
- Support dynamic headers for MCP servers via headersHelper configuration
- Fix thinking mode not working in headless sessions
- Fix slash commands now properly update allowed tools instead of replacing them

## 1.0.117

- Add Ctrl-R history search to recall previous commands like bash/zsh
- Fix input lag while typing, especially on Windows
- Add sed command to auto-allowed commands in acceptEdits mode
- Fix Windows PATH comparison to be case-insensitive for drive letters
- Add permissions management hint to /add-dir output

## 1.0.115

- Improve thinking mode display with enhanced visual effects
- Type /t to temporarily disable thinking mode in your prompt
- Improve path validation for glob and grep tools
- Show condensed output for post-tool hooks to reduce visual clutter
- Fix visual feedback when loading state completes
- Improve UI consistency for permission request dialogs

## 1.0.113

- Deprecated piped input in interactive mode
- Move Ctrl+R keybinding for toggling transcript to Ctrl+O

## 1.0.112

- Transcript mode (Ctrl+R): Added the model used to generate each assistant message
- Addressed issue where some Claude Max users were incorrectly recognized as Claude Pro users
- Hooks: Added systemMessage support for SessionEnd hooks
- Added `spinnerTipsEnabled` setting to disable spinner tips
- IDE: Various improvements and bug fixes

## 1.0.111

- /model now validates provided model names
- Fixed Bash tool crashes caused by malformed shell syntax parsing

## 1.0.110

- /terminal-setup command now supports WezTerm
- MCP: OAuth tokens now proactively refresh before expiration
- Fixed reliability issues with background Bash processes

## 1.0.109

- SDK: Added partial message streaming support via `--include-partial-messages` CLI flag

## 1.0.106

- Windows: Fixed path permission matching to consistently use POSIX format (e.g., `Read(//c/Users/...)`)

## 1.0.97

- Settings: /doctor now validates permission rule syntax and suggests corrections

## 1.0.94

- Vertex: add support for global endpoints for supported models
- /memory command now allows direct editing of all imported memory files
- SDK: Add custom tools as callbacks
- Added /todos command to list current todo items

## 1.0.93

- Windows: Add alt + v shortcut for pasting images from clipboard
- Support NO_PROXY environment variable to bypass proxy for specified hostnames and IPs

## 1.0.90

- Settings file changes take effect immediately - no restart required

## 1.0.88

- Fixed issue causing "OAuth authentication is currently not supported"
- Status line input now includes `exceeds_200k_tokens`
- Fixed incorrect usage tracking in /cost.
- Introduced `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` and `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL` for controlling model aliases opusplan, opus, and sonnet.

## 1.0.86

- Added /context to help users self-serve debug context issues
- SDK: Added UUID support for all SDK messages

## 1.0.85

- Status line input now includes session cost info

## 1.0.84

- Fix tool_use/tool_result id mismatch error when network is unstable
- Fix Claude sometimes ignoring real-time steering when wrapping up a task
- @-mention: Add ~/.claude/\* files to suggestions for easier agent, output style, and slash command editing

- @-mention: Support files with spaces in path
- New shimmering spinner

## 1.0.82

- SDK: Add request cancellation support

- Settings: Validation prevents invalid fields in .claude/settings.json fi
